popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'New York University' (legacyId=675) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official NYU Alumni profile pages, prioritizing person-specific alumni stories with explicit school/year evidence in the page title and substantive post-graduation impact described on NYU Alumni pages.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official NYU Alumni profile pages on nyu.edu/alumni.

items.name: Ada Limón
items.bio: NYU Alumni says U.S. Poet Laureate Ada Limón (GSAS ’01) wrote an original poem for NASA’s Europa Clipper mission, and that the poem is engraved on a plaque carried aboard the spacecraft.
items.description: U.S. Poet Laureate whose work was included on NASA’s Europa Clipper mission.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit GSAS ’01 class-year evidence in the title and a substantive career/achievement summary in the page body.

items.name: André De Shields
items.bio: NYU Alumni says André De Shields (GAL ’91) is a Broadway superstar known for The Wiz, The Full Monty, and the Tony- and Grammy-winning musical Hadestown.
items.description: Broadway performer celebrated for award-winning stage work including Hadestown.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit GAL ’91 class-year evidence and a substantive career summary in the profile metadata.

items.name: Kristoffer Diaz
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Kristoffer Diaz (GAL ’99, TSOA ’22) is a Pulitzer Prize-finalist playwright and the book writer of Hell’s Kitchen, which reached more than one million audience members and expanded to a national tour.
items.description: Pulitzer-finalist playwright and Hell’s Kitchen book writer.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit GAL ’99 and TSOA ’22 class-year evidence in the title and a substantive career summary in the page body.

items.name: Betty Weinberg Ellerin
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Betty Weinberg Ellerin (WSC ’50, ALW ’52) packed more legal punch than most of New York’s legal titans, highlighting her standing as a formidable figure in the law.
items.description: Trailblazing jurist and prominent legal figure in New York.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit WSC ’50 and ALW ’52 class-year evidence and a strong official profile description summarizing her legal stature.

items.name: Vanessa O’Brien
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Vanessa O’Brien (SPS ’91, STERN ’97) is the first woman to reach Earth’s highest and deepest places.
items.description: Explorer who became the first woman to reach Earth’s highest and deepest points.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit SPS ’91 and Stern ’97 class-year evidence and a concise official achievement summary.

items.name: Francisco Núñez
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Francisco Núñez (STEINHARDT ’88) founded the Young People’s Chorus of New York City, which grew from humble beginnings to global recognition including honors at the White House.
items.description: Founder of the Young People’s Chorus of New York City.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit Steinhardt ’88 class-year evidence and a substantive leadership/impact summary in the profile metadata.

items.name: Kimberly Seals Allers
items.bio: NYU Alumni says FemTech founder Kimberly Seals Allers (CAS ’96) moved from a successful journalism career into maternal-health work focused on taking bias out of birth.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit CAS ’96 class-year evidence and a substantive official summary of her journalism-to-FemTech career path.

items.name: Zachary Carter
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Zachary Carter (LAW ’75) was drawn to NYU Law for its clinical programs and commitment to social justice, and describes the unrest of the 1970s as an opportunity for him to enact change.
items.description: Law alumnus and public-interest legal leader shaped by NYU’s social-justice tradition.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit Law ’75 class-year evidence and a substantive official summary emphasizing his reform-oriented legal career.

items.name: Nily Rozic
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Assemblywoman Nily Rozic (CAS ’07) grew up with the motto ‘Leave your city greater than you found it,’ framing her public-service career in state government.
items.description: New York assemblywoman focused on civic service.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit CAS ’07 class-year evidence and a person-specific summary identifying her as Assemblywoman Nily Rozic.

items.name: Elizabeth Wiederseim
items.bio: NYU Alumni says Elizabeth Wiederseim (TSOA ’07), named to Variety’s Power Women of Hollywood list, is a driving force in entertainment whose work has helped guide actors including Colman Domingo, Will Brill, Nate Mann, and Leo Woodall.
items.description: Hollywood talent executive recognized on Variety’s Power Women of Hollywood list.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official NYU Alumni profile page with explicit TSOA ’07 class-year evidence and a substantive career summary in the article body.

warnings: NYU alumni depth was manually improved because the generic pipeline had shallow alumni coverage.
This override uses official NYU Alumni profile pages with explicit school/year evidence in page titles where available.
